audits.cleanReason: Reviewed the chapter's live contrast-negation candidates ("not to the air but to the pressure inside it," "We are not with the dead," "Not if he's already laid hands on the bastard," "Not what I mean," and "Not here"). Each survived as spatial precision, binary diagnostic correction, or hard-constraint dialogue, so no negation-addiction KILLs remained.

findings.line: The physical trail had already shown itself.
findings.subject: trail
findings.realAgent: The bootprints, sled grooves, and broken harness that daylight makes visible on the ground.
findings.reason: The sentence launders visibility onto the trail itself instead of naming the evidence Sylvanas is reading.

findings.line: A trail this blunt meant obsession.
findings.subject: trail
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas's reading of the broad, brutal track and the damage around it.
findings.reason: The trail is made to deliver judgment instead of keeping the inference with Sylvanas or the concrete signs she reads.
findings.suggestedFix: Only an obsessed commander left a trail this blunt.

findings.line: fingers trying to finish a pattern the blade kept shifting under her
findings.subject: fingers
findings.realAgent: Jaina, who is still trying to finish the pattern while Frostmourne's field shifts under her working hand.
findings.reason: The phrase gives intent to Jaina's fingers instead of keeping the action with Jaina herself.
findings.suggestedFix: she kept trying to finish the pattern as the field shifted under her

audits.cleanReason: Counted all chapter-level tic families. No corner-of-mouth beats or overloaded marked uses appeared; tightened/eased stayed below cap with the teeth-ache beat carrying concrete body sensation; non-said dialogue tags stayed at two ("muttered," "murmured"); and no eye/gaze or weight-metaphor cluster crossed the guide thresholds.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A found three isolated mild intent-smear/body-part-agency leftovers, each with a clean conservative fix and no moderate or severe findings.
